letters.label: Sighting Forty-One
variants:
Sighting Forty-One.

Witness: Edda Maer. Carriage 114. Long aboard.

Reports: figure in the doorway. Eyes white. Train at full motion. Five seconds. Gone.

Cross-questioned three times. Story did not move. Detail held.

The witness will not retract. Forty entries did. This may be the forty-first.

- H. Pent

Sighting Forty-One.

A man across from me asked what I was writing.

I said: field notes.

He said: about what.

I said: the white-eyed man.

He laughed.

It was the laugh of a person who has never seen him. I have catalogued the laugh.

- H. Pent

letters.label: Sighting Forty-Three
variants:
Sighting Forty-Three.

Witness: Pell Cordoba. Insists.

I offered her: lamp afterimage. Carriage flicker. Eye fatigue from the tunnels.

She said: I have lived underground. I know lamps.

I offered her: a man wearing eye protection. Some mining cults do.

She said: he had no shadow. The lanterns made shadows. He did not.

I closed the entry without an explanation.

This is unusual for me.

- H. Pent

Sighting Forty-Three.

Today I have interviewed four. Independent. No cross-contamination.

Carriage 87: white eyes through the door glass.

Carriage 154: white eyes through the door glass.

Carriage 226: white eyes through the door glass.

Carriage 401: white eyes through the door glass.

I have written the same sentence four times.

Four different witnesses wrote it before me.

- H. Pent

letters.label: Sighting Forty-Eight
variants:
Sighting Forty-Eight.

Witness: Joren Vask. Carriage 519.

Could not finish the interview. Twice put down the pen and stared at the floor.

Three witnesses today have done this. Same posture. Same delay.

Hypothesis: prolonged tunnel exposure produces a recurring hypnagogic figure. The white eyes are the afterimage of carriage lamps on the cone cells.

The trauma is real. The figure is not.

This is the most coherent theory I have constructed in years.

- H. Pent

Sighting Forty-Eight.

Working hypothesis.

Sustained tunnel-flicker. Disrupted circadian rhythm. Shared narrative exposure between strangers.

These conditions produce a folie a deux at scale. The white-eyed man is the train's local hallucination. Every long-haul population invents one.

The trauma is real. The figure is shared because the conditions are shared.

This is the entry I have been waiting to write for eleven years.

- H. Pent

letters.label: Sighting Sixty-One
variants:
Sighting Sixty-One.

I entered Carriage 47. A woman was eating.

I knew before she looked up.

The way she held the spoon. The way her eyes did not move toward the door glass when the train flickered.

I sat down. I said: when did you see him.

She said: yesterday.

The interview took two minutes.

I am very good at this.

- H. Pent

Sighting Sixty-One.

The signs I now recognise:

The flinch at carriage flicker. The half-second of held breath at a door opening. The avoidance of door glass.

The way they hold their cup with two hands.

I enter a carriage and read the room.

Three witnesses today. None disagreed with my opening question.

Eleven years. This is the year it has all paid off.

- H. Pent

letters.label: Sighting Seventy-Eight
variants:
Sighting Seventy-Eight.

Witness: Sora Penn. Carriage 488.

Claims she has seen nothing.

She has the spoon-hold. The door-glass avoidance. The half-second of held breath.

She has every sign in my notebook.

I thanked her for her time. I did not contradict her.

She wanted to be left thinking she was fine.

I left her thinking that.

- H. Pent
